steps.title: Identify the pattern needing explanation
steps.content: The two human-caused sound conditions produced negligible differences in their effects on narwhal diving, despite one containing an additional acoustic element (sonic pulses). Why doesn't the added stimulus create an additional behavioral change?

steps.title: Match the explanation to the choices
steps.content:
The matching choice explains this directly: ship sounds alone contribute so much to the overall sound level that adding sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals' auditory environment. If the auditory environment is not meaningfully changed by the additional pulses, the behavioral response would remain essentially the same.

The other choices either discuss acoustic propagation properties (A, irrelevant to why responses are similar), contradict the finding by describing an actual difference (C), or explain the overall magnitude of the effect rather than why two different exposures produce identical responses (D).

B is correct.
